title: Recursive problem design

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Recursive problem design; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Recursive design identifies a base case, smaller subproblem, combination rule, and progress measure that reaches termination.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Tree height returns zero for empty and one plus the maximum height of child subtrees otherwise. Your goal is to design and implement a terminating recursive algorithm from a recursive structure. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Readiness, algorithms and analysis; Array representation and operations; Selected-language readiness; pf abstraction recursion. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Recursive design identifies a base case, smaller subproblem, combination rule, and progress measure that reaches termination. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: C and C++ expose finite native call stacks without guaranteed tail-call elimination; Java likewise has bounded stack frames; Python has a comparatively low recursion limit, so all four implementations need an iterative alternative when depth scales with input.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Overlapping work, deep recursion, mutable shared state, and incorrect base coverage can make a valid-looking recurrence fail. The draft therefore rejects: Any recursive call with a numerically smaller argument guarantees correctness.

includes: Identify a base case, smaller subproblem, combination step and progress measure.
Design and implement a terminating recursive algorithm from a recursive structure.

C and C++ expose finite native call stacks without guaranteed tail-call elimination; Java likewise has bounded stack frames; Python has a comparatively low recursion limit, so all four implementations need an iterative alternative when depth scales with input.
excludes: Overlapping work, deep recursion, mutable shared state, and incorrect base coverage can make a valid-looking recurrence fail.

outcomes: Design and implement a terminating recursive algorithm from a recursive structure.

examples.title: Recursive problem design: implementation transfer
examples.situation: C and C++ expose finite native call stacks without guaranteed tail-call elimination; Java likewise has bounded stack frames; Python has a comparatively low recursion limit, so all four implementations need an iterative alternative when depth scales with input.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: C and C++ expose finite native call stacks without guaranteed tail-call elimination; Java likewise has bounded stack frames; Python has a comparatively low recursion limit, so all four implementations need an iterative alternative when depth scales with input.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Overlapping work, deep recursion, mutable shared state, and incorrect base coverage can make a valid-looking recurrence fail.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: Any recursive call with a numerically smaller argument guarantees correctness.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Recursive problem design, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
Overlapping work, deep recursion, mutable shared state, and incorrect base coverage can make a valid-looking recurrence fail.

recap.summary: You should now be able to design and implement a terminating recursive algorithm from a recursive structure. Keep this boundary in view: Overlapping work, deep recursion, mutable shared state, and incorrect base coverage can make a valid-looking recurrence fail.

Why is this claim false: Any recursive call with a numerically smaller argument guarantees correctness.
Map checkpoint 'combination rule' to C, C++, Java, and Python.
